verdict.summary: Guide is broadly on the right topic, but it contains a serious incorrect future/nationality-law statement, likely outdated AIMA fee figures, and several CPLP-specific caveats that are too vague or missing.

issues.current: Новый закон о гражданстве (Decreto AR 48/XVII, промульгирован Президентом António José Seguro 03.05.2026; вступит в силу после публикации в DRE) увеличит стаж до гражданства с 5 до 7 лет для всех граждан CPLP.
issues.problem: As of April 2026 this is wrong/misleading: the nationality law was approved on 01.04.2026 but was awaiting promulgation/publication. The guide states a later promulgation date and presents the change as more certain than it was. It also mixes an April-2026 source date with a May-2026 event.
issues.suggestion: Write: «Новый закон о гражданстве был одобрен парламентом 01.04.2026, но на дату проверки ожидал промульгации Президентом и публикации в DRE. До вступления в силу продолжает применяться действующее правило о 5 годах законного проживания; после вступления нового закона срок для граждан CPLP может измениться до 7 лет — проверяйте финальный текст в DRE.»
issues.source: Decreto da Assembleia da República 48/XVII; developer-provided April 2026 status

issues.current: По действующему праву натурализация после 5 лет законного проживания. **03.05.2026 Президент Сегуру промульгировал Decreto AR 48/XVII** — после публикации в DRE срок для CPLP увеличится до 7 лет
issues.problem: Same incorrect/unverifiable future statement. As of April 2026 the law was not yet promulgated. The guide should not tell users that promulgation already happened or that the exact new rule is already operative.
issues.suggestion: Keep the current-law sentence, then add a caveat: «В апреле 2026 парламент одобрил изменение закона о гражданстве, которое ещё ожидало промульгации и публикации. До вступления в силу действует срок 5 лет; после реформы для CPLP ожидается 7 лет, но нужно проверять финальную редакцию закона.»
issues.source: Decreto da Assembleia da República 48/XVII; Lei da Nacionalidade current regime

issues.current: AIMA — приём + анализ: amountEUR 133, note: с 01.03.2026
issues.problem: The guide gives a precise 2026 AIMA fee but does not cite the new fee table, and the amount appears to be an old/rounded value rather than the value under the 01.03.2026 tariff table. Sensitive immigration-fee numbers should be tied to the actual Portaria and checked against the 2026 IAS-based table.
issues.suggestion: Recheck and replace with the exact amount from the AIMA fee table effective 01.03.2026. Add the legal source in sources, e.g. «Portaria 480-A/2025/1 — tabela de taxas AIMA, em vigor desde 01.03.2026». If the site calculates dynamically, write «confirmar na tabela de taxas AIMA antes do pagamento» instead of a hard-coded amount.

issues.problem: The amount is likely outdated after the AIMA fee table change effective 01.03.2026 and is not supported by a cited tariff source. The guide should not present a precise 2026 residence-permit fee without the relevant Portaria/table reference.
issues.suggestion: Recalculate from the AIMA tariff table in force from 01.03.2026 and cite the legal act. If the amount is scenario-dependent, split «analysis/receção», «emissão do título», and any urgency/printing/postage components, or state that fees depend on the AIMA table applicable on the appointment/payment date.

issues.current: В AIMA большая очередь — записи могут быть на 2–4 месяца вперёд. Бронируйте время приёма заранее.
issues.problem: This is too optimistic and can mislead users. For AIMA/CPLP appointments and card issuance, delays can exceed 4 months; availability also varies heavily by channel, office, and whether the person already has a visa/appointment.
issues.suggestion: Write more cautiously: «В AIMA возможны существенные задержки: запись и выпуск карты могут занимать от нескольких месяцев и дольше, в зависимости от офиса, типа процедуры и доступности слотов. Проверяйте официальный канал AIMA и не планируйте поездки/смену работы исходя из гарантированных 2–4 месяцев.»

issues.current: timelineDaysMin: 30, timelineDaysMax: 120; «Обычно 30–90 дней. Карточка приходит по почте.»
issues.problem: The timeline is presented as a normal expectation but is too narrow for AIMA/CPLP in 2026. Appointment scarcity, biometric capture, production/backlog and postal delivery can make the total process much longer than 120 days.
issues.suggestion: Change to a non-guaranteed range: «После успешной подачи карта может прийти за 1–4 месяца, но из-за очередей AIMA и производства карт процесс нередко занимает дольше. Сроки не гарантированы.» Also distinguish appointment wait time from post-appointment card issuance.

issues.current: Категория — мобильность CPLP или тип D по ситуации. Типы документов — см. vistos.mne.gov.pt.
issues.problem: This is confusing: D1/D2/D3/D4/D6/D7/D8 are different residence-visa routes with their own requirements and usually lead to the corresponding residence-permit basis, not automatically to a CPLP mobility residence permit. Users may think any type-D visa can be treated as «CPLP».
issues.suggestion: Clarify: «Если цель — именно мобильность CPLP, запрашивайте визу/процедуру CPLP, когда она доступна в консульстве. Если подаётесь на D1/D2/D3/D4/D6/D7/D8, это отдельный маршрут с отдельными требованиями и последующим ВНЖ по соответствующему основанию, а не автоматический CPLP.»

issues.current: AIMA — Acordo de Mobilidade CPLP, url: https://aima.gov.pt/
issues.problem: The source is only the AIMA homepage, not the specific CPLP page or fee/procedure page. For an immigration guide with fees and procedural claims, this is too weak and may become unhelpful if the homepage changes.
issues.suggestion: Use the specific AIMA CPLP/mobility page if available, and add a separate source for the AIMA fee table effective 01.03.2026.

issues.current: No source for AIMA fee table effective 01.03.2026
issues.problem: The guide includes exact AIMA amounts and says they apply from 01.03.2026, but the legal basis for the new fee table is missing.
issues.suggestion: Add a source entry for «Portaria 480-A/2025/1 — tabela de taxas AIMA» or the official AIMA tariff page that reflects that Portaria.

issues.problem: Internal chronology is confusing: the change date is October 2025, sources were retrieved in April 2026, but the summary asserts a May 2026 promulgation. If the guide is meant to be current as of April 2026, the May 2026 claims should not be there.
issues.suggestion: Align metadata with the actual verification date and remove future-dated legal claims. If verified after May 2026, update sources with May retrieval dates and cite the DRE publication; otherwise keep the April 2026 status.

issues.problem: This may be misleading as a universal requirement. For residence-card procedures AIMA normally captures biometrics/photo at the appointment; some offices/checklists may not require a separate printed 3x4 photo.
issues.suggestion: Write: «Фото — только если указано в актуальном чек-листе/запросе AIMA; обычно биометрия и фото снимаются на приёме.»

missing_topics: Clear caveat that old electronic CPLP certificates/authorizations and the newer physical residence cards are not the same document, and Schengen travel/recognition may differ.
Specific source for the AIMA fee table effective 01.03.2026, not just the AIMA homepage.
Explanation that D1/D2/D3/D4/D6/D7/D8 are separate visa routes and should not be conflated with the CPLP mobility route.
Practical warning that appointment wait time and card-production time are separate and both can be delayed.
Caveat that nationality-law changes approved in April 2026 were not operative until promulgation and DRE publication.
